description: You are an agricultural economist at the Iowa Department of Agriculture conducting the 2022 Corn Yield Gap Analysis for state extension services. Using the workbook 'iowa_corn_yield.xlsx' in the ExcelTasks folder on the Desktop, complete the 'Yield_Gap_Analysis' sheet. For each county, calculate the 'Yield Gap (%)' in Column E as the percentage of the potential yield represented by the difference between potential and actual yields. In Column F, categorize each county as 'High Gap' (13% or greater), 'Low Gap' (less than 9%), or 'Medium Gap'. In Column G, rank the counties from 1 (smallest gap) to 22 (largest gap) based on their Yield Gap %. In cell E24, calculate the state area-weighted average yield gap using the harvested area and the yield gap percentages. Save the completed workbook to its original location.

metadata.dataset: Real 2022 Iowa county corn yields from USDA NASS (Feb 24 2023); ISU Extension district benchmarks for potential yields
